title: Neonatal Seizures from HIE
chief_complaint: Term infant with difficult delivery (shoulder dystocia, low Apgars: 1, 3, 5). Now having rhythmic jerking of left arm and face with eye deviation.

physical_exam.Neurologic: Subtle seizures: Rhythmic jerking of left upper extremity, lip smacking, eye deviation to left. Hypotonia. Poor suck. Moro reflex absent.

hidden_data.eeg: Seizure activity: Right temporal lobe epileptiform discharges.
hidden_data.mri_brain: Watershed infarcts in right MCA/ACA border zone. Basal ganglia/thalamus hyperintensity on T1 (severe HIE pattern).
hidden_data.electrolytes: Normal Na, Ca, Mg (rules out electrolyte cause)
hidden_data.abg: pH 7.08, PaCO2 55, PaO2 50, Lactate 10.2 (severe metabolic acidosis from HIE)

correct_diagnosis: Neonatal Seizures secondary to Moderate-Severe Hypoxic-Ischemic Encephalopathy (HIE) - Sarnat Stage 2
key_learning_points: Neonatal seizures often SUBTLE (lip smacking, eye deviation, bicycling movements, apnea) - NOT generalized tonic-clonic due to immature brain myelination
HIE = most common cause of neonatal seizures. Other causes: Hypoglycemia (MUST check glucose first), Hypocalcemia, Infection, Intracranial hemorrhage, Inborn errors of metabolism, Drug withdrawal
Therapeutic hypothermia: Cooling to 33.5°C for 72 hours within 6 hours of birth. Reduces mortality and neurodevelopmental disability in moderate-severe HIE (NNT=7)

Anti-epileptics: Phenobarbital 20mg/kg IV load (first-line). Fosphenytoin 20mg PE/kg if refractory. Levetiracetam increasingly used. Benzodiazepines for acute cessation. Maintain for 3-6 months then wean if seizure-free and normal EEG
Prognosis: Sarnat Stage 1 = normal outcome 95%. Stage 2 = CP/cognitive deficits 25-50%. Stage 3 = mortality 50%, severe disability in survivors
